01_initial_request.input_data: CHO cells displaying the target antibody gene
01_initial_request.user_intent: To increase mutation diversity and affinity maturation efficiency by introducing adenine deaminase (ADA) to induce A/T to G/C mutations in the CHO display system

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Previously established CHO cell display platform for antibody affinity maturation using AID. [Gap] AID-induced mutations are limited to G/C to A/T conversion, restricting mutation diversity. [Decision] To increase mutation diversity, chose to use molecular cloning to construct pCEP4 plasmids expressing different deaminases (mAID-plus, TadA-7.10, TadA-8e, and fusion variants) on target antibody gene, expecting to obtain multiple enzyme expression systems for comparison.

parameters.enzyme_types: mAID-plus, TadA-7.10, TadA-8e, TadA-7.10-mAID-plus, mAID-plus-TadA-7.10, double promoter configuration
parameters.NLS: SV40 NLS added to N- and/or C-termini

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Seven pCEP4 plasmids constructed and confirmed by sequencing with no errors introduced

references.relevance: Previous work describing mAID-plus plasmid construction
Original TadA-7.10 creation for gene editing
TadA-8e development with higher adenine deamination efficiency

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Need to verify if ADA can induce random A/T to G/C mutations in CHO cells. [Gap] Unknown whether ADA works for random mutation without Cas9 guidance. [Decision] To test mutation capability, chose to use flow cytometry (FACSAriaIIIu) to detect GFP reverse mutation on GFP-TAG plasmid transfected with deaminase plasmids, expecting to obtain fluorescence ratios indicating mutation efficiency.

parameters.transfection_mixture: 1μg pCDNA3.1-GFP-TAG, 1μg pCEP4 expression plasmids, 5μL PEI MAX
parameters.NLS_configurations: N-terminus NLS only, N- and C-termini NLS

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Fluorescence ratios: AID with N-terminus NLS 1.33%, two-termini NLS 0.964%; ADA with N-terminus NLS 4.28%, two-termini NLS 4.73%

references.relevance: GFP-TAG plasmid system for reverse mutation detection

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] GFP-TAG test showed different mutation efficiencies for different NLS configurations. [Gap] Need to select optimal NLS configuration for subsequent experiments. [Decision] To optimize mutation efficiency, chose to use decision analysis to select NLS-AID (N-terminus only) and NLS-ADA-NLS (two-termini) based on fluorescence ratios, expecting to obtain best-performing enzyme configurations for affinity maturation.

parameters.selection_criteria: Higher fluorescence ratio indicates better mutation efficiency

02_agent_trajectory.observation: NLS-AID and NLS-ADA-NLS selected for later experiments

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Optimal NLS configurations identified. [Gap] Need to test single deaminase affinity maturation on anti-TNFα scFv. [Decision] To assess mutation and affinity improvement, chose to use PEI MAX transfection to introduce mAID-plus, TadA-7.10, or TadA-8e plasmids into CHO cells displaying anti-TNFα scFv, expecting to obtain mutant cell populations for screening.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Three cell samples transfected with different deaminases for affinity maturation

references.relevance: CHO cell-based display system for antibody maturation

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Cells transfected with deaminase plasmids. [Gap] Need to accumulate mutations through cell proliferation and select for high-affinity binders. [Decision] To enrich high-affinity mutants, chose to use cell culture with G418 selection to expand cells for 11-13 days, expecting to obtain sufficient cell population for FACS enrichment.

parameters.medium: IMDM with 10% FBS, HT, and 1mg/mL G418 disulfate

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Cells expanded to 10^8 (S1 cells) with accumulated mutations

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] S1 cells expanded with accumulated mutations. [Gap] Need to enrich cells with highest antigen-binding ability. [Decision] To isolate high-affinity binders, chose to use FACSAriaIIIu for first round FACS enrichment, expecting to obtain 20,000 cells with strongest binding to GFP-TNFα.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: 20,000 cells enriched based on antigen-binding ability (S1 to S2 transition started)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] First FACS enrichment completed. [Gap] Need further enrichment to obtain highest affinity clones. [Decision] To further purify high-affinity binders, chose to use FACSAriaIIIu for second round FACS enrichment, expecting to obtain 2,000 cells (S2) with strongest binding.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: 2,000 cells enriched as S2 population for sequencing

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] S2 cells enriched with high-affinity mutants. [Gap] Need to identify mutation types and frequencies. [Decision] To characterize mutations, chose to use Sanger sequencing on 70 randomly selected scFv clones from genomic DNA, expecting to obtain mutation profiles for each deaminase.

parameters.PCR_primers: scFv-F and scFv-R with NheI and XhoI sites

02_agent_trajectory.observation: 69 mutations from mAID-plus, 58 from TadA-7.10, 56 from TadA-8e; AID mutations 98.55% on G/C bases, ADA mutations 100% on A/T bases

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Single deaminase results show complementary mutation patterns. [Gap] Need to test if combining AID and ADA increases mutation diversity. [Decision] To increase mutation types, chose to use PEI MAX transfection with 4 different AID+ADA combination plasmids (fusion enzymes, double promoters, double plasmids), expecting to obtain more diverse mutant libraries.

parameters.combination_types: 4 different combinations: TadA-7.10-mAID-plus fusion, mAID-plus-TadA-7.10 fusion, double promoters, double plasmids

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Four cell samples transfected with different AID+ADA combinations

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Combined deaminase cells transfected. [Gap] Need to enrich and characterize combined deaminase mutants. [Decision] To obtain high-affinity mutants from combined approach, chose to use FACSAriaIIIu for FACS enrichment (two rounds) followed by Sanger sequencing of 70 clones per sample, expecting to obtain mutation profiles showing increased diversity.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Mutations occurred on both A/T and G/C bases in all 4 samples; mutation frequencies and types close across 3 combinations, ADA-AID fusion slightly lower

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Sequencing identified 31 mutant scFv clones from combined and single deaminase samples. [Gap] Need to verify binding ability of individual mutants. [Decision] To assess antigen-binding abilities, chose to use PEI MAX transfection to transiently express wild-type and 31 mutant scFv genes in CHO cells, expecting to obtain cells displaying mutant antibodies for binding analysis.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: 29 of 31 scFvs successfully displayed on CHO cells, 2 failed to display

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Mutant antibodies displayed on CHO cells. [Gap] Need to quantify binding ability of each mutant. [Decision] To measure antigen-binding ability, chose to use FACSAriaIIIu to analyze GFP-TNFα binding, expecting to obtain GM_GFP values correlating with affinity.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: GM_GFP positively correlated with antigen-binding ability; Spearman's rank coefficient -0.802, P<0.001; all tested mutant KD values lower than wild-type

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Flow cytometry identified high-binding mutants. [Gap] Need precise affinity measurements (KD values). [Decision] To quantify antibody affinity, chose to use Octet Red96 biomolecular interaction technology to measure KD values of selected mutants, expecting to obtain quantitative affinity data for comparison with wild-type.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Wild-type KD: 2.48E-06; Top 3 mutant KDs: 4.65E-08 (A495G+A719T), 8.55E-08 (A719T), 8.22E-08 (A718T); all mutants showed lower KD than wild-type

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Octet biomolecular interaction technology (Octet Red96) with KD measurement

wild_type_KD.interpretation: Baseline affinity of unmutated anti-TNFα scFv

top_mutant_KD_1.interpretation: Best affinity achieved (A495G+A719T double mutant), 53-fold improvement over wild-type

top_mutant_KD_2.interpretation: Second best affinity (A719T single mutant), 29-fold improvement

top_mutant_KD_3.interpretation: Third best affinity (A718T single mutant), 30-fold improvement

mutation_frequency_AID.interpretation: Mutation frequency induced by mAID-plus on G/C bases

mutation_frequency_ADA_7.10.interpretation: Mutation frequency induced by TadA-7.10 on A/T bases

mutation_types_AID.interpretation: Number of different mutation types from AID alone

mutation_types_combined.interpretation: Combined AID+ADA produced more mutation types than either alone

correlation_coefficient.interpretation: Strong negative correlation between GM_GFP and KD (P<0.001), validating FACS screening

03_success_verification.final_verdict: Research objective achieved. ADA successfully induced random A/T to G/C mutations in CHO cell display system, complementing AID's G/C to A/T mutations. Combined use of AID and ADA increased mutation diversity and types. All tested mutant antibodies showed higher affinity (lower KD) than wild-type, with top mutants achieving 29-53 fold affinity improvement. The platform efficiently matured antibody affinity through increased mutation diversity.
